title: Tool use lifecycle: define, call, execute, return

body.concept: Tool use follows a four-step cycle. (1) Define: you describe tools via JSON Schema (name, parameters, description) and send them in the API request. (2) Call: Claude analyzes the user's request and decides which tool(s) to call, returning tool_use blocks with structured arguments. (3) Execute: your application runs the actual code (for client tools) or Anthropic runs it (for server tools). (4) Return: the tool result is sent back as a tool_result message, and Claude uses it to produce its final answer. This cycle can repeat — Claude may chain multiple tool calls before answering.
body.analogy: Like a doctor (Claude) writing a prescription (tool_use). The doctor decides what tests to order but never draws blood — the lab (your app) runs the test and sends results back. The doctor then interprets the results and advises the patient.
body.visual: You → [tools + user message] → Claude → [tool_use block] → Your app executes → [tool_result] → Claude → [final answer]. Loop back to tool_use if more calls needed.
